title: CivFolio
summary: The strategy-map resume site and open-source portfolio framework that turns project history into a living world.
description: CivFolio is the meta-city in the empire: the portfolio framework itself. It takes Phil's own body of work and renders it as a 4X-inspired world map, with projects as cities, Great Works as landmarks, and growth over time as a campaign.

It is intentionally both a personal artifact and a reusable open-source starter. That makes it part demo, part framework, and part statement about how software portfolios can feel authored instead of interchangeable.

media.alt: The CivFolio world map with the CivFolio city management view open.
media.caption: The live CivFolio map acting as its own city-management screen.

media.alt: A stylized world-map city representing the CivFolio framework.
media.caption: A self-referential city where the portfolio becomes the framework.

greatWorks.title: Empire Cartographer
greatWorks.summary: A portfolio framework that turns otherwise static career history into an explorable living world.

relationships.label: Career capital represented as game world

highlights: Transforms a resume and project archive into a strategy-map experience.
Ships as a self-hosted, file-backed open-source framework instead of a closed personal site.
Combines interaction design, data modeling, and visual worldbuilding in one product.
productionQueue: Expand the starter so other creators can drop in their own data and projects.
Deepen the world simulation with richer units, landmarks, and era transitions.
Polish the map UX until it feels as smooth as a small strategy game.

architectureNotes: Designed as a self-hosted portfolio starter with no database dependency.
Uses a typed content layer so the personal demo can also act as a reusable framework.
